name: Alberta (Canada)

colors.symbolism: the snow-capped mountain peaks and the sky on the shield
the Cross of Saint George in the chief of the shield
the wheat field on the shield
the foothills and prairie on the shield

description: An ultramarine blue field with the Alberta provincial shield centred; the shield shows the Cross of Saint George above a landscape of snow-capped mountains, foothills, prairie, and wheat.
history: Adopted on 1 June 1968, with proportions standardised in 1968. The shield in the centre is taken from Alberta's coat of arms, granted by Royal Warrant in 1907; the rest of the arms (crest, supporters, motto) was added in 1980 but is not on the flag.

features.role: shield of arms of Alberta (Cross of Saint George above mountains, foothills, prairie, and wheat)
features.symbolism: Alberta's coat of arms, granted in 1907: in chief a red Cross of Saint George on white (recalling English heritage), and below it a landscape with snow-capped Rocky Mountains, green foothills, prairies, and a yellow wheat field.
